Convert moderate-version-conflicts/Inventory.slnx to Central Package Management. Use the highest version for any conflicts.

input.initialPrompt: Convert moderate-version-conflicts/Inventory.slnx to Central Package Management. Use the highest version for any conflicts.

data.reasoningText:
Let me start by understanding the task. I need to convert a .NET solution using `moderate-version-conflicts` to Central Package Management (CPM). This involves:

1. Understanding the current project structure and package references
2. Creating a `Directory.Packages.props` file with centralized versions
3. Removing `Version` attributes from individual project files
4. Using the highest version when there are conflicts

Let me first explore the directory structure and understand what we're working with.

data.content:
Now I can see the conflicts. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` and update all project files.

**Conflicts resolved (highest version wins):**
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 vs 8.0.4 → **10.0.1**
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 vs 1.11.4 → **1.13.2**

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information. Let me identify the packages and their versions across projects:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests |
|---------|-----|--------|--------|-------|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - |


| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 |

I've spotted version conflicts: System.Text.Json has 10.0.1 in most places but 8.0.4 in Worker, and Azure.Identity differs between 1.13.2 and 1.11.4. I'll resolve these by using the higher versions across the board. My plan is to set up centralized package management by creating a Directory.Packages.props file with all the resolved versions, then strip out the version attributes from each project file.

    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
